text:
The Iranian military has been carrying out projectile attacks against tankers in the area, setting ships ablaze and sending the price of crude oil surging on world markets. It has also begun laying mines in the strait, despite the United States destroying some Iranian ships capable of that type of operation.
Mr. Wright said in another interview on Sunday that the strait was not safe for tanker passage. “No, it is not,” he said on NBC’s “Meet the Press.”
About 20 percent of the world’s oil goes through the narrow waterway connecting the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman.
The average price for a gallon of gas in the United States has hit $3.699, up from $3.45 a week ago and $2.927 a month ago, the nonprofit motoring group AAA said on Sunday.

title: Oil Prices May Not Fall Soon, U.S. Energy Secretary Says
